'pi' carries the .pi card, 'execNote'/'band'/'llm' the model verdict. Full reference + comment threads at joeycastillo.us/metal/pi. tilde.html renders this file directly.

tildes.name: New Project
tildes.oneLiner: Say a name once and get the whole real project — folder, git, scaffold, private repo, landing page — born production-shaped, not a toy, and land inside it ready to work.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want one command to mint the WHOLE project — folder, git on main, the be/ ledger, the kernel doc, the deploy stub, the proposal site, the private GitHub repo — so the first thing that exists is already the real thing, not a starter kit I'll rebuild later. As a builder, I want it to check every sibling org for a name twin BEFORE it creates anything, so a project has exactly one home and I never repeat the two-repos-one-name mistake — fell that way once, never again. As a builder, I want it to refuse to overwrite a folder that already exists, so a typo can't destroy real work. As an operator, I want this to run free with no model in the loop when I give it full args, so minting a project costs nothing and comes out identical every time — the only conversation is when I left the org out or a name collides. As a builder, I want a GitHub hiccup to leave my local work intact, so a network fall never costs me the project.
tildes.aiDoes: Sanitizes the name, checks the folder doesn't exist, runs git init and gh repo create, writes the be/ scaffold + CLAUDE.md + deploy.json + proposal/index.html with placeholders substituted, commits, pushes, switches the working directory into the project, and prints an inline Created summary.
tildes.execNote: 90% Hot: with full args every step is a scripted action-unit; the org-pick/collision dialogue is the sole escalation. Corrects tildes.json's “sonnet” — no standing model needed.

---
Be right the first time: the project you mint IS the project you ship.

tildes.name: List Projects
tildes.oneLiner: See everything you've got — every project on this machine, every Be repo in your orgs, every open handoff — in one glance, free, no guessing.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As an operator, I want a single command that shows me every local project, every Be repo across my orgs, and every open transfer in four clean tables, so I know the whole estate at a glance instead of remembering it. As an operator, I want this to run free with no model in the loop, so taking the census never costs a cent — every field is read straight from git, GitHub, and the files themselves, so nothing on the screen can be made up. As an operator, I want remote repos listed only when GitHub confirms they actually carry a be/ ledger, so the list is signal, not noise. As an operator, I want open handoffs to show "(none)" rather than vanish, so an empty section is a fact I can trust, not a hole I have to wonder about.
tildes.aiDoes: Walks C:\* directories, runs git status/log per repo, reads telemetry tails for Last Run, calls gh repo list and gh api for be/ detection on remotes, scans be/transfers/, then prints the four-section table inline. No commit, no report.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot: a census in a costume; every field is a git/gh/file read formatted into a fixed table. Corrects tildes.json's “haiku” — zero model needed.

tildes.name: Load Repo
tildes.oneLiner: Say a project's name and be standing in it — cloned if missing, synced only when safe, next move recommended, live board on screen — free, and it never bulldozes your work.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As an operator, I want to say a name — a URL, a path, a nickname — and be inside that project ready to work, cloned if it isn't here yet, so switching projects is one breath, not a ritual. As a builder, I want it to pull ONLY when my tree is clean and never push, so loading a project can never bulldoze work in progress — that's a fall nobody gets to take twice. As an operator, I want it to hand me the next board item without moving anything, so the second I land I know the move — the recommendation is free advice, ~b makes it real. As an operator, I want the live board popped in my browser and a warning if another session is already in this project, so a collision is visible before it becomes a conflict. As an operator, I want all of this free with no model in the loop — the name is resolved by a vocabulary it actually knows, so an unknown name fails loud instead of guessing, and a guess can never send me into the wrong repo.
tildes.aiDoes: Determines the local path, clones if missing, reads git state, conditionally pulls when clean, switches the working directory, computes a Next-item recommendation from board.md, prints the inline Loaded summary, and launches the live kanban board view in a browser.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot, already shipped Tier-0 in metal-de r257: resolution, sync guards, Next-item walk, board pop, and presence are all rule-table mechanics. Corrects tildes.json's “haiku” — no model in the loop.

tildes.name: Inspect
tildes.oneLiner: Point it at anything — a repo, a URL, a folder — and get the honest story of what it is, how it's built, and what's still unanswered, with every claim backed by the files that prove it.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want to point at any repo, URL, or folder and get the real story — what it is, how it's shaped, what patterns it carries, what's still open — so I start work knowing the terrain instead of discovering it mid-climb. As a builder, I want every judgment call in the report to cite the files that back it up, so I'm never handed a confident wrong answer — never trust the monkey; show me, don't tell me. As an operator, I want the counting half — the structure, the stack, the file census — done free by pure machinery, so the money only goes where actual judgment lives: what it is, what patterns it carries, what's unanswered. As a builder, I want the read to be strictly look-don't-touch, so inspecting a thing can never change the thing — its own published report is the only write. As the next session, I want the Open Questions section to be where board work gets born, so discovery flows straight into the lineup instead of evaporating.
tildes.aiDoes: Fetches/reads the target, synthesizes a structured inspect report, writes the HTML to be/plans/, links it from be/index.html, commits + pushes, logs telemetry with verb 'inspected in', and pops the report for the user.
tildes.execNote: Cold-lean: the whole tilde IS the deep-read-plus-synthesis judgment; only the render/commit tail is mechanical. Matches tildes.json — no correction.

tildes.name: Unload
tildes.oneLiner: Close out a project the way a pro leaves a job site — every loose end found and shown to you, nothing decided behind your back, the status report shipped either way, and you're back home.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As an operator, I want the close-out to hunt every loose end — uncommitted changes, unpushed commits, items still marked In Progress, a deploy that trails the code — so nothing gets left dangling because nobody looked. As an operator, I want each finding shown to me with plain choices — commit, stash, or leave; push or leave; move back or leave — and NOTHING committed, pushed, or thrown away without me saying so, because losing work to an eager robot is a fall we never take. As the next session, I want the status report to ship every single time, clean exit or messy one, so whoever loads this project next reads exactly where it stands instead of guessing. As an operator, I want this to run free with no model standing by — the audit and the menus are pure machinery; I supply the judgment, and the session-work prose can always degrade to the raw commit list at $0.
tildes.aiDoes: Reads git + board + deploy state, offers interactive cleanup choices, renders the unload status HTML, updates the index, commits + pushes any approved housekeeping, switches the working directory back, and prints the inline Unloaded summary.
tildes.execNote: 92% Hot: audit, menus, report, and context switch are scripted; the only slot is Session-Work summary prose, degradable to a raw commit list at $0. Corrects tildes.json's “sonnet” — no standing model needed.

tildes.name: Design
tildes.oneLiner: Design the whole production thing at once — go big, make the hard 10% look easier than the first 90%, and de-risk testing in production by rehearsing it (TLT on a rope, TLD in the mind) then shipping.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want the design to target production from the first line — the real thing, not a phase-1 — so I'm building for real while everyone else is celebrating 90%. As a builder, I want the hard 10% designed to look easier than the first 90%, because anyone reaches 90%; the last 10% is the whole job. As a builder, I want to test in production on production-like data, de-risked the way a free-soloist is — rehearse it on a rope (TLT, the-last-test), rehearse the moves in my mind (TLD, the-last-demo), then execute (ship). As a builder, I want to fall fast and never repeat a mistake — fuck up as fast as possible, then never that way again, staying open to new falls if they make me faster. As a builder, I want fast + cheap + right at the same time, because today the magic makes all three real and I'm not afraid to try. No baby steps. Be right the first time.
tildes.aiDoes: Names the real need, drafts raw user stories, sources the approach from a-d-d + kastil-systems/nous (C99 primitives) + existing apps + external prior art before inventing, proposes literal names (metaphor only when accurate; brand names exempt), shapes the minimum viable structure, and defines the smallest First Move that proves it — writing the dated HTML to be/designs/ from the leaner template, updating the index, committing + pushing, logging telemetry with verb 'designed for', and popping the design. Small designs skip the optional analytical tools. Writes no code.
tildes.execNote: Cold — the only three real judgments are the root, the hard 10%, and the rehearsal plan; everything else is theater. fable executes; the mining + scaffold tail stays C99, $0.

tildes.name: Look and Feel
tildes.oneLiner: Say how the thing should feel and get the whole production look back — the rules, the behavior, the ready-to-paste styles — for free when your words are already in the book.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want to say "calm, dense, premium" and get the ENTIRE look-and-feel back at once — not a mood board, not a phase-1 palette — so the production skin exists before the first screen does. As an operator, I want my words matched against a table we own first, so that describing the feel costs nothing — the machine only asks for help on a word it's never seen, and once it learns that word, it never asks again. As an operator, I want the free path to be the front door: when I name the feel explicitly, no model runs at all — $0. As a builder, I want contradictions caught and resolved before anything renders — keep the two traits that matter most, bend the rest toward them — because a design that argues with itself is the 10% everyone else skips. No named styles, ever. We roll our own.
tildes.aiDoes: Collects or infers the 6 axes, runs the constraint layer to resolve conflicts, fills the 4 output sections plus a CSS :root block into the template, writes the dated HTML to be/designs/, updates the index, commits + pushes, and walks the user through the intent flagging any inferred axis.
tildes.execNote: 70% Warming trending Hot: the 1,620-cell constraint engine and output tables are pure C99; the sole slot is axis classification, a light-classify job. Corrects tildes.json's fable — renting deep judgment for a table lookup plus a classifier is the exact waste the scoring exists to catch. Explicit-axes invocations bypass the slot entirely = $0.

tildes.name: Plan
tildes.oneLiner: Write the plan as a bet you expect to revise — and force yourself to name, out loud, what you don't know yet and the exact signal that sends you back.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want the plan to aim at production from line one — the real thing, phases and constraints for the real thing — so the plan and the ship are the same shape. As a builder, I want to be FORCED to write down what I don't know yet, because naming the unknowns is the one judgment no machine and no replay can do for me — an empty Unknowns section means I haven't thought hard enough, and that's where the fall is hiding. As a builder, I want every plan to carry its own trigger for v2 — the concrete signal that says "you've learned something, go rewrite" — so falling fast is built into the paper, not bolted on after. As the next session, I want v2 to supersede v1 mechanically — new filename, old one marked, newest always loads — so nobody ever builds from a dead plan. Plans are hypotheses. A plan that survives the whole project unrevised is suspect.
tildes.aiDoes: Writes the structured plan including the mandatory Unknowns and When-To-Revisit sections, versions the filename if superseding, renders the HTML to be/plans/, links it from the index, commits + pushes, logs telemetry with verb 'planned for', and pops the plan for approval.
tildes.execNote: 30% Cold; the Unknowns slot is floor=cold FOREVER (naming what you don't know is irreducible judgment) and the approach is real synthesis. Confirms tildes.json's fable; only the render/version/commit tail goes C99.

tildes.name: Estimate
tildes.oneLiner: Break the plan into pieces sized for one perfect night of work, stack them into boards you can actually finish, and put the most valuable piece on top.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want every piece of work cut to a 3 or a 5 — a 5 is one college-student night, complete and perfect — so there's never a piece too big to be right the first time. As a builder, I want any piece that isn't a clean single move — one command, one way to prove it — rejected and re-cut, so no fuzzy blob ever lands on a board looking like work. As an operator, I want the most valuable thing first, always, because sell for show, ship for dough. As the machine, I want every piece written as {what, verb, context} — subject, predicate, object — so the board itself is something I can read, query, and reason over, not prose I have to guess at. As a builder, I want any piece that touches a new host, port, or service to automatically bring its firewall-and-verify sibling with it — ports don't open themselves, and forgetting one is a mistake we've already fallen on once.
tildes.aiDoes: Reads the plan, decomposes/recomposes into 3/5-pt triples, groups and orders boards, writes be/board.md, renders the estimate HTML to be/plans/, updates the index, commits + pushes (board.md alongside), logs telemetry with verb 'estimated for', and pops the report.
tildes.execNote: 55% Warming; structured decomposition into triples is the slot and sonnet is the cheapest-capable model for it. Confirms tildes.json's sonnet. Sizing back-loads: as telemetry accumulates duration truth per verb/context, the 3-vs-5 call becomes a C99 table lookup.

tildes.name: Plan User Story
tildes.oneLiner: Say the work as a story a human feels — who wants it, what they want, why — with proof conditions sharp enough to run as tests, not read as prose.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want the work framed as who/what/why in the user's own words, so the thing I build is the thing a person actually feels — not a spec nobody asked for. As a builder, I want every acceptance criterion written as something that can PASS or FAIL on its own — "user can X" has to resolve to a check a machine can run — so the story walks straight into testing with no translation step. That's the rope: the criteria I write here are the same holds TLT rehearses before the ship. As an operator, I want the story reviewed the moment it's written, before anything downstream moves, so a wrong story falls fast and cheap instead of expensive and late. The story is the spec; the criteria are the test plan wearing story clothes.
tildes.aiDoes: Drafts the As-a/I-want/So-that story plus acceptance criteria, renders the HTML to be/plans/, reviews the story, updates the index, commits + pushes, and pops the report.
tildes.execNote: 45% Warming; acceptance-criteria drafting is structured decomposition (each criterion must be a binary-checkable oracle), sonnet is cheapest-capable. Confirms tildes.json's sonnet; the As-a/I-want/So-that frame itself is grammar the tail can hold.

tildes.name: Plan Test
tildes.oneLiner: Decide what to prove before anything gets built — the obvious cases for free, and the failures nobody wrote down imagined on purpose, then kept only if they're real.
tildes.spec: USER STORIES ONLY. As a builder, I want the test plan written BEFORE the tests exist — what to prove, where it breaks, what it leans on — because rehearsing the fall before the climb is the whole point of the rope. As an operator, I want every failure shape we've ever seen replayed for free — the library answers first, and the machine only imagines edges the library has never met. As a builder, I want the imagining pointed at the 10% the spec never named — the falls nobody predicted — because anyone can test the happy path; the last 10% is the job. As an operator, I want a hard gate on what the library learns: an imagined edge gets in only with a concrete way to trigger it, or it's stamped HYPOTHESIS and kept out — imagination alone never pollutes the free replay path. Never trust the monkey. As a builder, I want every real new edge to make the next run cheaper — fall once, learn it forever, never that way again.
tildes.aiDoes: Writes the test-strategy plan covering cases, edge cases, and dependencies, renders the HTML to be/plans/, reviews the strategy, updates the index, commits + pushes, and pops the report.
tildes.execNote: 40% Cold today: edge imagination is deep adversarial judgment, so the model IS the executor. Corrects tildes.json's sonnet — under-renting the one slot that exists to see what the spec didn't. Cold→Hot by design: every novel edge graduates into an edge-rule library, so replay against known shapes trends to $0.
